name: AlgebraicJacobian_Picard_TensorObjInverse.lean.md
content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjInverse.lean ‚Äî iter-103 (prover, prove)

## Objective
Close the LAST keystone sorry (c) `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (target) by proving the
iter-103 3-seam decomposition (Seam1 `trivialisation_restrict_eM_split`, Seam2
`trivialisation_telescope_assemble`, Seam3 `trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise`) and assembling
the target.

## Result summary
- **Seam 2 `trivialisation_telescope_assemble` ‚Äî CLOSED (PROVEN, building, axiom-clean).** The generic
  abstract-`[Category C]` œÅ-cancellation cocycle collapse (sibling of `dual_scp_assemble`/`unit_assemble`/
  `hcore_assemble`). This is the planner's designated seam-confiner ("confines ALL `SheafOfModules ‚â´`
  seam-crossing", applied by `exact`). Verified in isolation first (pure Mathlib category theory) then
  in-file (`lake build ‚Ä¶TensorObjInverse` EXIT 0, 8567 jobs).
- **NEW keystone helper `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` ‚Äî TYPED + partial proof (`apply Iso.ext`) + sorry.**
  This is the genuine Seam-1 ingredient the directive/blueprint reference as
  "`restrictIsoUnitOfLE hVU eM = (restrict j) eM` modulo the unit identifications". Statement is
  type-verified (build EXIT 0). `apply Iso.ext` reduces it to the `.hom` chart-chase (goal captured below).
- **Seam 1 `trivialisation_restrict_eM_split` / Seam 3 `trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise` ‚Äî NOT created.**
  Their exact reindexings (Seam1) / section-level eqToHom threading (Seam3) require interactive goal
  inspection which is impossible here (LSP DEAD on this monster-importing file ‚Äî every `lean_goal`/
  `lean_diagnostic` call returned `Broken pipe`). Adding them blind risked breaking the build.
- **Target `trivialisation_restrict_compat` ‚Äî UNCHANGED (sorry retained).** Its first real step is the
  section descent (Seam3) which cannot be done blind.

## `trivialisation_telescope_assemble` (Seam 2) ‚Äî RESOLVED
### Approach
Generic 5-square telescope over abstract `[Category C]`: six reindexing isos `œÅ0‚Ä¶œÅ5`, five
`restrict j`-image constituents `dU1‚Ä¶dU5`, five V-constituents `cV1‚Ä¶cV5`, hypotheses
`dU_k = œÅ_{k-1}.hom ‚â´ cV_k ‚â´ œÅ_k.inv`, conclusion
`dU1 ‚â´ ‚Ä¶ ‚â´ dU5 = œÅ0.hom ‚â´ (cV1 ‚â´ ‚Ä¶ ‚â´ cV5) ‚â´ œÅ5.inv`.
### Proof
`subst h1 h2 h3 h4 h5; simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]`.
The adjacent `œÅ_k.inv ‚â´ œÅ_k.hom = ùüô` cancellations fire as `Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc`. **Key fix:** initial
direction was wrong (`œÅ.inv ‚â´ cV ‚â´ œÅ.hom` gives a type mismatch ‚Äî `dU_k : O_{k-1} ‚ü∂ O_k`, so the correct
conjugation is `œÅ_{k-1}.hom ‚â´ cV_k ‚â´ œÅ_k.inv`). Verified.

## `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` (Seam-1 keystone) ‚Äî PARTIAL
### Statement (type-verified)
```
restrictIsoUnitOfLE hVU e
  = restrictCompReindex j hjŒπ M ‚â™‚â´ (restrictFunctor j).mapIso e ‚â™‚â´ unitRestrictIso j
```
(j arbitrary open immersion with `j ‚â´ U.Œπ = V.Œπ`; forced `= Scheme.Hom.resLE (ùüô X) U V hVU` by `U.Œπ` mono.)
### Residual goal after `apply Iso.ext` (captured via build `trace_state`)
```
‚ä¢ (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hVU e).hom
    = (restrictCompReindex j hjŒπ M ‚â™‚â´ (restrictFunctor j).mapIso e ‚â™‚â´ unitRestrictIso j).hom
```
- **LHS** (unfold `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`) = pullback-world chain
  `RFIP(V.Œπ) ‚â´ pullbackCongr(hjŒπ.symm) ‚â´ (pullbackComp j U.Œπ).inv ‚â´ (pullback j)(RFIP(U.Œπ).inv)
   ‚â´ (pullback j) e ‚â´ pullbackObjUnitToUnit j`.
- **RHS** (unfold `restrictCompReindex`/`unitRestrictIso`) = restrict-world chain
  `restrictFunctorCongr(hjŒπ).inv ‚â´ restrictFunctorComp j U.Œπ ‚â´ (restrictFunctor j) e ‚â´ RFIP(j)
   ‚â´ pullbackUnitIso j`.
### Blocker
The `restrictFunctorComp ‚Üî pullbackComp` reconciliation through the `pullbackCongr`/`restrictFunctorCongr`
shim. The bridge IS available (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback_comp_general`, the same RFIP-bridge S2/S4c use)
but threading it requires build-driven `trace_state` stepping at every reduction (LSP dead) ‚Äî ~20 blind
1.5-min builds, not feasible this session. This is the re-break candidate per the no-grind guard.

## `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (target) ‚Äî UNCHANGED
The target is section-level (`.val.app` over `U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V` with outer `eqToHom`s from
`image_preimage_of_le`). Its assembly needs Seam1 (the `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` keystone above,
not yet proven) + Seam2 (now available) + Seam3 (section descent, not created). The section descent ‚Äî the
bridge between `(restrict j M).val.app` and the chart's sections ‚Äî is the conceptually hardest part and
cannot be developed without interactive goal inspection. Body unchanged (extensive blueprint plan retained).

## Dead ends / environment notes
- **LSP is DEAD on this file** (confirmed: `lean_goal`, `lean_diagnostic_messages`, `lean_run_code` all
  return `[Errno 32] Broken pipe` ‚Äî the file imports the 12.8M-HB monster). Use build `trace_state` only.
- `lean_run_code` needs a prior LSP call to set the project path; it never succeeded here.
- Seam2 initial conjugation direction `œÅ.inv ‚â´ cV ‚â´ œÅ.hom` is a TYPE ERROR ‚Äî use `œÅ.hom ‚â´ cV ‚â´ œÅ.inv`.

## Needs blueprint entry
- `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` (private, `TensorObjInverse.lean`, before Seam2) ‚Äî NEW helper, no
  blueprint block. Statement: `restrictIsoUnitOfLE hVU e = restrictCompReindex j hjŒπ M ‚â™‚â´
  (restrictFunctor j).mapIso e ‚â™‚â´ unitRestrictIso j`. Uses (intended): `restrictFunctorIsoPullback_comp_general`,
  `restrictCompReindex`, `unitRestrictIso`, `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`. This is the Lean form of the blueprint's
  "`restrictIsoUnitOfLE hVU eM = (restrict j) eM`" identity that `lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split`
  (Seam 1) rests on. Reviewer/planner: add a `\label`+`\lean`+`\uses` block (likely a sub-lemma of
  `lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split`).

## Summary
- **Sorry count: 1 ‚Üí 2.** Pre-existing target sorry `trivialisation_restrict_compat` UNCHANGED; one NEW
  decomposition sorry `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` (correctly typed, partial proof `apply Iso.ext`,
  residual goal documented). The increase is a genuine decomposition: the new sorry is a named, typed
  sub-obligation the target needs, extracted from the monolith.
- **Closed (proven, sorry-free): `trivialisation_telescope_assemble` (Seam 2).**
- **Still open:** `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (target ‚Äî needs Seam1+Seam3, not blind-doable);
  `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` (Seam-1 keystone ‚Äî chart-chase blocked on dead LSP).
- Adjacent sorries beyond assigned: none attempted (the only other file sorry is the dead
  `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` dup, out of my domain).

## Why I stopped
**Partial progress.** Closed Seam 2 `trivialisation_telescope_assemble` (the planner's designated generic
seam-confiner) ‚Äî a new proven, building, axiom-clean helper; and added + reduced the Seam-1 keystone
`restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` to a concrete `.hom` chart-chase goal (captured, documented). Did NOT
close the target. Specific blocker: the file's LSP is dead (every interactive goal query returns Broken
pipe), and the remaining two seams (Seam1 reindexings, Seam3 section descent) plus the keystone chart-chase
each need step-by-step interactive goal inspection that here costs a full ~1.5-min `trace_state` build per
step ‚Äî closing any of them would take ~15‚Äì20 blind builds, exceeding this session. Per the no-grind guard,
left clean documented sorries rather than grinding the monolith. Did not write any approach as a comment
without attempting it (Seam2 attempted+closed; keystone attempted via `Iso.ext`+goal capture).

name: blueprint-writer-leaf-split.md
content:
# Blueprint Writer Report: leaf-split
**Status:** COMPLETE

## Changes
- Add `% archon:covers .../TensorObjSubstrate/TrivialisationRestrict.lean` (covers list, after PullbackTensorMapIso).
- Add `lem:restrictfunctorisopullback_comp_general` (`...restrictFunctorIsoPullback_comp_general`): general literal-composite B2 bridge (no congr legs); notes `_hom` = its `.hom`. Placed after `def:unitrestrictiso`. `\uses{def:restrictfunctorisopullback, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}`.
- Add `lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict` (`...restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict`): Seam-1 keystone, before `lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split`. `\uses{def:restrictcompreindex, def:unitrestrictiso, lem:restrictfunctorisopullback_comp_general}`.
- Wire `lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict` into `lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split` `\uses{}` (statement + proof).
- L1 (`dual_restrict_iso_comp`) NOTE: removed stale `% PLANNER` directive; updated "un-blueprinted (coverage debt)" ‚Üí now blueprinted at the general block.

## Notes / Strategy
- leandag: no `unknown_uses`; both new nodes non-isolated; all crefs resolve. No external sources (all project-internal glue).

name: lean-auditor-iter103.md
content:
# Lean Audit Report

## Slug
iter103

## Iteration
103

## Scope
- files audited: 1
- files skipped (per directive): 0

---

## Per-file checklist

### AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjInverse.lean
- **outdated comments**: 4 flagged
- **suspect definitions**: 0
- **dead-end proofs**: 0
- **bad practices**: 0
- **excuse-comments**: 0
- **notes**:
  - **`trivialisation_telescope_assemble` (L2631‚Äì2644): VERIFIED sorry-free and not vacuous.**
    Proof: `subst h1 h2 h3 h4 h5; simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]`.
    After `subst`, each `dU_k` is replaced by `œÅ_{k-1}.hom ‚â´ cV_k ‚â´ œÅ_k.inv`; `Category.assoc`
    right-normalises both sides; `Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc` cancels each `œÅ_k.inv ‚â´ œÅ_k.hom` pair,
    leaving `œÅ0.hom ‚â´ cV1 ‚â´ cV2 ‚â´ cV3 ‚â´ cV4 ‚â´ cV5 ‚â´ œÅ5.inv` on each side. Not vacuous: the
    abstract `[Category C]` pattern is the standard seam-crossing design (mirrors `dual_scp_assemble`,
    `unit_assemble`, `hcore_assemble`); the 12 objects, 5 morphisms, 6 isos, and 5 hypotheses are
    all independently constrained. Generic but non-trivial.
  - **`restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` (L2591‚Äì2613): NEW decl, `apply Iso.ext; sorry`.**
    Signature: given `hVU : V ‚â§ U`, `j : V ‚ü∂ U` `[IsOpenImmersion j]`, `hjŒπ : j ‚â´ U.Œπ = V.Œπ`,
    `e : M.restrict U.Œπ ‚âÖ ùí™_U`, states
    `restrictIsoUnitOfLE hVU e = restrictCompReindex j hjŒπ M ‚â™‚â´ (restrictFunctor j).mapIso e ‚â™‚â´ unitRestrictIso j`.
    This is the Seam-1 keystone claim ("e ‚Ü¶ (restrict j) e" identification). The docstring
    accurately describes the goal. The `apply Iso.ext; sorry` is a genuine open proof skeleton
    (not a vacuous body). Comment at L2609 ("BLOCKER: the `restrictFunctorComp ‚Üî pullbackComp`
    reconciliation ... needs build-driven `trace_state` stepping (LSP dead on this monster-importing
    file); re-break candidate per the no-grind guard") is a proof-planning note for the sorry.
  - **`trivialisation_restrict_compat` (L2654‚Äì2723): remains `sorry`** ‚Äî consistent with the docstring
    at L2700‚Äì2722 describing the five-constituent telescope plan.
  - **L1146 (stale cross-reference):** Comment says "see the `dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat`
    sorry below". `dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` (L1484‚Äì1514) is NOW sorry-free (closes with
    `simp only [Iso.trans_assoc]; rfl`). The "sorry below" reference is stale.
  - **L2394‚Äì2398 (stale BLOCKER):** Comment says "BLOCKER for executing now: exposing
    `STU ‚â´ a_Y(pbv‚äópbv) ‚â´ whisk` as a contiguous subterm...". The same block at L2399 then says
    "EXECUTED (iter-066)" and the proof (L2403‚Äì2421) is sorry-free. The stale BLOCKER text was not
    pruned after execution, creating a misleading contradiction within the same comment block.
  - **L804‚Äì805 (stale residual label):** STATUS comment says lemma body "rides transitively only the
    single reduced residual `sheafPullbackUnit_forget_eq`". `sheafPullbackUnit_forget_eq` (L459‚Äì704)
    is now proven (ends with `exact Eq.trans...`, no `sorry`). Calling it a "residual" is stale.
  - **L556 (stale ESCALATION):** Comment says "ESCALATION (per PROGRESS iter-052): mathlib-analogist
    cross-domain on `ofIsRightAdjoint`-unit transparency / the `pullbackIso ‚Üî sheafificationCompPullback`
    coherence". The `hKEY` sub-proof it describes is proven in-line at L525‚Äì691. Stale.
  - **L1825 (orphaned cross-reference):** "iter-099 UPDATE: the iter-085 BLOCKER above is RESOLVED."
    The "BLOCKER above" was rewritten / replaced; the current surrounding text has no BLOCKER, making
    this UPDATE annotation confusing (it refers to removed content).
  - **Two active sorries:** L2613 (`restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict`) and L2723
    (`trivialisation_restrict_compat`). Both are genuine open proofs; neither is a vacuous body or
    wrong definition.
  - **No unauthorized axioms or `Classical.choice` instances.** `:= rfl` appears only on
    definitional-equality lemmas (L306, L627, L863, L2188) ‚Äî appropriate.
  - **No suspect bodies.** All public lemma bodies that terminate are sorry-free.

---

## Must-fix-this-iter

None.

No excuse-comments on load-bearing definitions; no weakened-wrong definitions; no unauthorized axioms;
no suspect bodies (the two `sorry`s at L2613 and L2723 are genuine open proofs, not vacuous
definitions or wrong statements). The `trivialisation_telescope_assemble` closure is sound.

---

## Major

- `TensorObjInverse.lean:1146` ‚Äî Comment says "see the `dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` sorry below"
  but `dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` (L1484‚Äì1514) is now sorry-free. Misleads a reader about
  the file's sorry count and proof state.

- `TensorObjInverse.lean:2394` ‚Äî Stale "BLOCKER for executing now" text contradicts the "EXECUTED
  (iter-066)" marker at L2399 and the sorry-free proof body (L2403‚Äì2421) in the same comment block.
  A reader sees "BLOCKER" and may incorrectly believe the proof is blocked.

- `TensorObjInverse.lean:804` ‚Äî STATUS comment still calls `sheafPullbackUnit_forget_eq` a
  "reduced residual" after it has been proven (L459‚Äì704). Misleads about transitively-open sorries.

- `TensorObjInverse.lean:556` ‚Äî "ESCALATION (per PROGRESS iter-052)" for the `hKEY` sub-proof is
  stale: `hKEY` is proven in-line at L525‚Äì691 with no sorry. Leaves dead cross-domain escalation
  text pointing at closed work.

---

## Minor

- `TensorObjInverse.lean:1825` ‚Äî "iter-099 UPDATE: the iter-085 BLOCKER above is RESOLVED" is an
  orphaned annotation: the BLOCKER it updated has been replaced/rewritten, so the UPDATE now refers
  to text that no longer exists in the file.

- `TensorObjInverse.lean:2609` ‚Äî "re-break candidate per the no-grind guard" inside
  `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` ‚Äî internal project jargon that reads as a deferral marker
  for the sorry; low priority since the sorry's presence is already explicit.

- `TensorObjInverse.lean:2715` ‚Äî "BLOCKER (iter-040 finding..." in `trivialisation_restrict_compat`
  accurately describes the mathematical obstacle but mixes old iteration-timeline cross-references
  with the current proof state, adding noise without corresponding sorry justification value.

---

## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)

None. The BLOCKER comments at L2609 and L2715 explain technical constraints or mathematical obstacles
for open proofs; they do not admit that a definition is wrong or that a placeholder is in use.
They do not meet the auditor's excuse-comment threshold ("admission that the code is wrong").

---

## Severity summary

- **must-fix-this-iter**: 0
- **major**: 4
- **minor**: 3
- **excuse-comments**: 0

Overall verdict: The file is in a sound state ‚Äî `trivialisation_telescope_assemble` is genuinely
sorry-free and correctly proven, `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` is a well-typed new keystone
skeleton, and the two remaining sorries are legitimate open proofs. Four stale comments
(L1146, L2394, L804, L556) misrepresent the current proof state and should be pruned.

name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker-iter103.md
content:
# Lean ‚Üî Blueprint Check Report

## Slug
iter103

## Iteration
103

## Files audited
- Lean: `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjInverse.lean`
- Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` (consolidated `% archon:covers ‚Ä¶ TensorObjInverse.lean` chapter)

Scope per directive: the three-seam decomposition of `lem:trivialisation_restrict_compat`
(Seam-2 `trivialisation_telescope_assemble`, Seam-1 `‚Ä¶_em_split`, Seam-3 `‚Ä¶_sectionwise`) and the
new helper `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict`. Narrow file-vs-chapter view; not a full-file audit.

## Per-declaration

### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.trivialisation_telescope_assemble}` (chapter: `lem:trivialisation_telescope_assemble`, Seam-2)
- **Lean target exists**: yes ‚Äî `private lemma trivialisation_telescope_assemble` at `TensorObjInverse.lean:2631`.
- **Signature matches**: yes. Abstract `{C : Type*} [Category C]`, six objects each side, five constituents `dU1‚Ä¶dU5` / `cV1‚Ä¶cV5`, six reindex isos `œÅ0‚Ä¶œÅ5`, five square hypotheses, telescoping conclusion `dU1 ‚â´ ‚Ä¶ ‚â´ dU5 = œÅ0.hom ‚â´ (cV1 ‚â´ ‚Ä¶ ‚â´ cV5) ‚â´ œÅ5.inv`. This is exactly the abstract "five squares + four internal œÅ cancel telescopically, two outer œÅ survive" shape the blueprint block specifies. Sibling of `dual_scp_assemble`/`unit_assemble`/`hcore_assemble` as the prose claims.
- **Proof follows sketch**: yes ‚Äî `subst h1 h2 h3 h4 h5; simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]`. Sorry-free / genuinely CLOSED (verified by inspection; matches the blueprint's "pure `Category.assoc` cocycle collapse, applied by `exact`, no seam crossed"). Directive's "claimed CLOSED" is accurate.
- **notes**:
  - *Orientation convention is mirrored (minor).* The blueprint writes square `k` as `(restrict j) c^U_k = œÅ_{k-1}^{-1} ; c^V_k ; œÅ_k` (inverse on the **left**, hom on the **right**) and the conclusion as `œÅ_0^{-1} ; (‚Ä¶) ; œÅ_5`. The Lean writes `dU_k = œÅ_{k-1}.hom ‚â´ cV_k ‚â´ œÅ_k.inv` (hom on the left, inv on the right) and concludes `œÅ0.hom ‚â´ (‚Ä¶) ‚â´ œÅ5.inv`. The two are exact mirrors (relabel each `œÅ ‚Üî œÅ.symm`); the abstract lemma holds under either convention and both telescope correctly, so there is no correctness impact ‚Äî but a prover transcribing the blueprint literally would get the inverse placement flipped. Worth a one-line prose alignment.
  - *`\leanok` placement (informational).* The **statement** block carries `\leanok` (line 9929) ‚Äî correct, the declaration is formalized. The **proof** block (lines 9954‚Äì9964) has **no** `\leanok`, even though the Lean proof is sorry-free. `\leanok` is sync-managed and this is sync's domain, but the review agent may want to confirm the proof-block marker (the lemma is `private`; possible sync resolution lag).

### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.trivialisation_restrict_eM_split}` (chapter: `lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split`, Seam-1)
- **Lean target exists**: **no.** No declaration named `trivialisation_restrict_eM_split` exists in `TensorObjInverse.lean` or anywhere under `AlgebraicJacobian/` (grep repo-wide: NONE). Confirms the prover's "Seam-1 NOT created".
- **Signature matches**: N/A (no Lean target).
- **Proof follows sketch**: N/A.
- **notes**: Dangling `\lean{}` hint ‚Äî the blueprint references a Lean decl that does not yet exist. Correctly **unmarked** (no `\leanok` on statement or proof), so the chapter does not over-claim. This is a planned-but-unbuilt decomposition node, not shipped-wrong code. See "Blueprint adequacy" for the `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` overlap.

### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise}` (chapter: `lem:trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise`, Seam-3)
- **Lean target exists**: **no.** No declaration `trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise` anywhere under `AlgebraicJacobian/` (grep: NONE). Confirms "Seam-3 NOT created".
- **Signature matches**: N/A.
- **Proof follows sketch**: N/A.
- **notes**: Dangling `\lean{}` hint, correctly unmarked (no `\leanok`). Planned node.

### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.trivialisation_restrict_compat}` (chapter: `lem:trivialisation_restrict_compat`)
- **Lean target exists**: yes ‚Äî `private lemma trivialisation_restrict_compat` at `TensorObjInverse.lean:2654`.
- **Signature matches**: yes. The big eqToHom-conjugated sectionwise equation (U-chain over `U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V` vs V-chain over `V.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V`) is a faithful formalization of the prose "its further restriction along `V ‚Ü™ U` equals the same chain built directly over `V` ‚Ä¶ lifts to an equation of restricted sheaf morphisms over `V`".
- **Proof follows sketch**: **partial** ‚Äî body ends in `sorry` (`TensorObjInverse.lean:2723`). This is the keystone the whole seam decomposition serves. The blueprint marks the **statement** `\leanok` (line 9996, = formalized-with-sorry, correct) and leaves the **proof** block (10013‚Äì10035) **unmarked** (correct, proof open). So the markers accurately reflect "stated, not proved". The in-code TELESCOPE PLAN comment matches the blueprint's three-seam proof prose step-for-step.
- **notes**: Not a disguised placeholder ‚Äî the open `sorry` is honestly reflected by the unmarked proof block and is the explicit subject of this iteration's seam work.

## Red flags

### Placeholder / suspect bodies
- None that the blueprint claims complete. The two `sorry`s in the file (`restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` at line 2613; `trivialisation_restrict_compat` at line 2723) are genuine open obligations whose blueprint proof blocks are correctly left **unmarked**. No `:= sorry`/`:= True`/`:= rfl` masquerading as a closed substantive claim. (Other "sorry" hits at lines 804, 1146, 1156, 1827 are inside comment prose, not bodies.)

### Excuse-comments
- None. The in-code comments on the two sorry'd lemmas are accurate status/blocker notes ("BLOCKER: ‚Ä¶ re-break candidate per the no-grind guard"), not excuses for wrong code.

### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
- None. No `axiom` declarations in the file.

## Unreferenced declarations (informational)

- **`restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict`** (`TensorObjInverse.lean:2591`, NEW this iter): no `\lean{}` block anywhere in the chapter (grep: NO blueprint reference). **Substantive** ‚Äî it proves the Seam-1 keystone identity `restrictIsoUnitOfLE hVU e = restrictCompReindex j hjŒπ M ‚â™‚â´ (restrictFunctor j).mapIso e ‚â™‚â´ unitRestrictIso j`, currently `apply Iso.ext; sorry`. Flagged below as a coverage gap (this is the directive's second focus item).
- The other helpers in scope (`restrictCompReindex`, the `conjugateEquiv_*` legs, `restrictFunctorIsoPullback_comp_compat*`, etc.) are outside the directive's narrow Seam focus and several are already `\uses`-referenced; not re-audited here.

## Blueprint adequacy for this file

A bidirectional check restricted to the seam decomposition.

- **Coverage**: The three seam blocks point at Lean names; **1 of 3** resolves (`trivialisation_telescope_assemble`). The other two (`trivialisation_restrict_eM_split`, `trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise`) are dangling ‚Äî blueprint‚ÜíLean references to decls that do not exist yet. Separately, the keystone Seam-1 helper that **does** exist in Lean (`restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict`) has **no** `\lean{}` block ‚Äî a Lean‚Üíblueprint gap. Net: the Seam-1 layer is misaligned in both directions (blueprint names a decl the prover didn't build under that name; the prover built a decl the blueprint doesn't name).
- **Proof-sketch depth**: adequate. The em_split / telescope / sectionwise proof prose and the parent `trivialisation_restrict_compat` proof give a detailed, faithful preview of the in-code TELESCOPE PLAN. No under-specification surfaced.
- **Hint precision**: **loose** at Seam-1/Seam-3. The two dangling `\lean{}` hints pin names the Lean side never adopted. Recommend either creating Lean decls under those exact names, or correcting the hints to the names the prover actually lands.
- **Generality**: matches need (telescope_assemble is correctly abstract `[Category C]`).
- **Recommended chapter-side actions** (for a blueprint-writing subagent):
  1. **Add a `\lean{}` block for `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict`** as a sub-lemma feeding `lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split`. Assessment per directive: **yes, it should be a sub-lemma of Seam-1.** The em_split's first bullet *is* this identity ("the `e^M`-leg ‚Ä¶ `restrictIsoUnitOfLE h_{VU} e^M = (restrict j) e^M`"); the Lean helper proves exactly that factorization (modulo `restrictCompReindex`/`unitRestrictIso`). Currently em_split's `\uses` cites only `def:unitrestrictiso`/`def:restrictcompreindex` for this step ‚Äî promoting `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` to its own `\lean{}`-tagged block and adding it to em_split's `\uses` would close the gap.
  2. **Reconcile the Seam-1/Seam-3 `\lean{}` names** (`trivialisation_restrict_eM_split`, `trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise`) with whatever the prover ultimately names them ‚Äî they are dangling today.
  3. Optionally align the telescope_assemble prose orientation (`œÅ^{-1}` left / `œÅ` right) with the Lean (`œÅ.hom` left / `œÅ.inv` right) to avoid a literal-transcription trap.

## Severity summary

- **must-fix-this-iter**: none. No placeholder disguised as complete, no signature mismatch against pinned prose, no excuse-comments, no unauthorized axioms, and the blueprint is detailed enough to have guided this file. Both `sorry`s are open obligations the chapter honestly leaves unmarked.
- **major**:
  - Seam-1 `\lean{trivialisation_restrict_eM_split}` and Seam-3 `\lean{trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise}` are **dangling** ‚Äî blueprint references to non-existent Lean decls (confirms prover's "not created"). Expected in-progress state, but they are unresolved `\lean{}` targets.
  - **`restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` has no blueprint block** despite being the substantive Seam-1 keystone (Lean‚Üíblueprint coverage gap). Should be a `\lean{}`-tagged sub-lemma of `lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split`.
- **minor**:
  - telescope_assemble reindex-orientation convention is the mirror of the Lean (cosmetic, no correctness impact).
  - telescope_assemble **proof** block lacks `\leanok` although the Lean proof is sorry-free (sync's domain; flagged for the review agent to confirm).

Overall verdict: The Seam-2 telescope lemma is faithfully formalized and genuinely CLOSED, the parent and its two open `sorry`s are honestly reflected by the blueprint markers (no must-fix), but the Seam-1/Seam-3 layer is bidirectionally misaligned ‚Äî two dangling `\lean{}` targets plus the unreferenced keystone helper `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` ‚Äî which a blueprint-writing pass should reconcile.

content:
# Progress Critic: cfrontier-iter104
**Iter:** 104

## Routes

- **`TensorObjInverse.lean` ‚Üí (c) telescope assembly**: **CONVERGING**.
  - Sorry trajectory (K=4, iters 100‚Äì103): 3‚Üí2‚Üí1‚Üí2. Net ‚àí1 over window; the 1‚Üí2 bounce explained below.
  - Bounce breakdown: iter-103 CLOSED Seam2 (structural advance: generic œÅ-cocycle, axiom-clean) AND extracted `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` as a typed sub-sorry (structural decomposition revealing a concrete sub-obligation with documented chart-chase). Net ‚àí1, not net unchanged ‚Äî the two moves are at different proof nodes.
  - CHURNING check: helpers in ‚â•2 iters ‚úì; net <0.5/iter (borderline) ‚úó (net is exactly 1/4); **"no structural change"** UNMET ‚Äî the Seam1/2/3 decomposition + this iter's leaf-split ARE structural changes. Third condition not met ‚Üí CHURNING verdict does NOT apply.
  - Prover status: COMPLETE√ó3, then PARTIAL√ó1. PARTIAL was *mandated* by the no-grind guard in PROGRESS.md; this is not a churn-PARTIAL, it is a guard-PARTIAL.
  - Recurring blockers: "LSP DEAD / Broken pipe" appears in iter-103 only (first and only occurrence in K=4 window). NOT a 3-iter recurring blocker. This iter's proposed structural fix (leaf-split) directly addresses it.
  - Avoidance: none. No off-critical-path reclassification; no plan-only iters; no deferral language.
  - Throughput: ~1‚Äì2 iters estimated (STRATEGY.md), ~2‚Äì3 elapsed in current sub-phase (telescope assembly entered iter-102). Slipping ~1.5√ó; not 2√ó; no must-fix.
  - **Directive answer**: the +1-helper sorry is genuine extraction, NOT churn. `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict` is a correctly typed, documented sub-obligation whose sole blocker was the dead-LSP environment; the leaf-split refactor proposed this iter directly resolves that constraint.

## Dispatch Sanity
- **Verdict**: OK. 1 file dispatched (new leaf `TrivialisationRestrict.lean`); 1 sorry to close; no other files with open sorries except the TensorObjSubstrate dead-dup (cleanup candidate, not a prover target). Well under cap.

## Must-fix-this-iter
*(empty)*

## Overall
- 1 CONVERGING route, 0 CHURNING/STUCK, 0 avoidance findings, dispatch=OK. The iter-103 bounce (1‚Üí2) is a genuine extract-and-identify move under a no-grind guard, not a churn signal. Proceed with the leaf-split + prover on `restrictIsoUnitOfLE_eq_restrict`.
